name: WizardLM 7B
note: Original WizardLM: LLaMA fine-tuned on Evol-Instruct data, a method that seeded WizardCoder/WizardMath and much of the field.

results.notes: pass@1, per original WizardLM-7B-V1.0 model card
AlpacaEval win rate (%) vs text-davinci-003 baseline, per original model card table

verificationNotes: Confirmed real: arXiv 2304.12244 (submitted 2023-04-24) lists authors Can Xu, Qingfeng Sun, Kai Zheng, Xiubo Geng, Pu Zhao, Jiazhan Feng, Chongyang Tao, Qingwei Lin, Daxin Jiang, affiliated 1=Microsoft, 2=Peking University — org confirmed. Corrected: the official HF repo is WizardLM/WizardLM-7B-V1.0 (input had non-existent 'WizardLMTeam/WizardLM-7B-V1.0'; that org handle exists for the 13B/Math models but not this 7B one, and it 401'd/does not resolve). Corrected: the '78' score is NOT a 'vicuna-eval win rate vs Vicuna-13B' as the input claimed — the TheBloke-mirrored model card literally labels it 'AlpacaEval' win rate against the text-davinci-003 baseline, so I relabeled benchmarkSlug to alpacaeval and fixed the notes; HumanEval 19.1 pass@1 confirmed verbatim on the same page. Context length 2K is consistent with the LLaMA-1 base (2048 tokens) used in April 2023. License is not machine-readable on HF (tagged 'other') but is widely documented as non-commercial/research-only, inherited from Meta's original LLaMA-1 research license — kept with lower confidence.
